title: UI Portal Trading — Order Entry, Order Book & Security Search
problem_statement: An investor can sign in and fund an account through the portal but still cannot trade from the UI. There is no way to find a security, enter a market or limit order, or watch an order progress through its lifecycle on screen. Without trading screens, the core value of the product is unreachable to a real user.

goals.goal: Let an investor find and select securities quickly.
goals.success_measure: A typeahead search returns matching securities by ticker or name and lets the investor select one to trade.
goals.measurement_method: E2E asserting search results and selection populate the order form.

goals.goal: Let an investor place market and limit orders with clear feedback.
goals.success_measure: The order form supports market and limit buy/sell with client-side validation matching Section 15.2 and shows submission results including rejections mapped from RFC-7807.
goals.measurement_method: E2E covering a successful market buy and a rejected order (insufficient funds) with correct messaging.

goals.goal: Let an investor track and cancel orders.
goals.success_measure: An order book view lists orders with live-updating status and allows cancelling working orders, disabling cancel for terminal orders.
goals.measurement_method: E2E asserting an order transitions to FILLED and a working order can be cancelled while a terminal one cannot.

personas.name: Alex
personas.goal: Search a security, place an order, and watch it fill or reject.
personas.pain_point: Without trading screens the investor cannot actually buy or sell.

functional_requirements.description: Implement a security search/typeahead component querying the securities search API and showing current price, feeding selection into the order form, per Section 6.6.

Implement an order entry form supporting market and limit, buy and sell, with React Hook Form + Zod validation that mirrors the server rules (Section 15.2): quantity is an integer 1–10,000; limitPrice is required when type=LIMIT and must be absent when type=MARKET, within $0.01–$9,999,999.99 (2 decimal places). Show an estimated cost (quantity × price).
Submit orders to the orders API, handle the 202 accepted response, and surface rejections (insufficient-funds, position-insufficient, account-not-active) mapped from RFC-7807 per Section 15.3.
Implement an order book view listing the investor's orders with status, using TanStack Query polling/refetch on a 5-second interval so fills and rejections appear without a manual reload.
Allow cancelling PENDING/OPEN orders from the order book and disable/deny cancel for terminal orders, reflecting the 409 terminal-state response, per Section 15.3.

accessibility: The order form is keyboard-accessible with labelled inputs, and destructive actions (cancel) require clear affordance.
performance: Order status updates appear via polling/refetch every 5 seconds, which bounds API load while keeping status current.
security: All trading actions use the authenticated session; the UI never exposes another user's orders.

acceptance_criteria.given: an authenticated investor on the trading screen
acceptance_criteria.when: they type a partial ticker or company name
then: matching securities with current price are listed
selecting one populates the order form

acceptance_criteria.given: a selected security and sufficient buying power
acceptance_criteria.when: the investor submits a market buy for a valid quantity
then: the order is accepted and appears in the order book as PENDING

acceptance_criteria.scenario: Rejected order shows a clear reason
acceptance_criteria.given: insufficient buying power for the requested order
then: no order is added to the order book

acceptance_criteria.when: the fill is processed by the backend
then: the order book reflects the order as FILLED without a manual reload

acceptance_criteria.given: a working order in the order book
and: cancel is unavailable for terminal orders

in_scope: Security search/typeahead feeding the order form
Market and limit order entry with client-side validation and estimated cost
Order submission with RFC-7807 rejection handling
Order book view with live-updating status
Cancel working orders with terminal-state handling

out_of_scope.item: Advanced order types and charts on the trade ticket
out_of_scope.rationale: v1 supports only market and limit orders (BE-3 scope).

out_of_scope.item: Real-time streaming quotes in the UI via websockets/SSE
out_of_scope.rationale: v1 uses bounded polling/refetch; live quote streaming to the browser is out of scope.
Not part of the v1 initial feature set.

existing_patterns: App shell, routing, and auth handling from BE-8
TanStack Query polling/refetch for eventually-consistent order status
integration_points: Orders API: place, list, get, cancel (BE-3)
deployment_constraints: Runs within the Nginx-served UI container in the local stack

github_repos.notes: Trading UI (security search, order entry, order book) in /ui. Repo is created and scaffolded by BE-11 (devops-ci-cd-foundation).

spec_documents.label: BrokerEdge Design Document — Section 6.5/6.6 (UI/UX), Section 15.2 (validation), Section 15.3 (errors)
spec_documents.notes: Authoritative source for trading UX, order validation, and error handling.

risks.description: Order status is eventually consistent (async clearing), so the UI may briefly show stale status and appear broken.
risks.mitigation: Use TanStack Query polling with clear pending states and optimistic-but-verified updates.
risks.trigger: Users report an order stuck as PENDING in the UI after it filled.
risks.contingency: Add a manual refresh affordance and tune the poll interval.

risks.description: Client-side order validation diverges from backend business rules, allowing submissions that always fail.
risks.mitigation: Base Zod rules on Section 15.2 and treat backend RFC-7807 responses as authoritative.
risks.trigger: Orders accepted by the form are consistently rejected by the API.
risks.contingency: Tighten client rules to match server constraints.

internal.description: Core UI shell, routing, and authenticated API client.

definition_of_done: An investor can search for and select a security to trade.
An investor can place market and limit orders with clear validation and rejection feedback.
An investor can watch orders progress and see fills reflected without a manual reload.
An investor can cancel working orders and is prevented from cancelling terminal ones.
Trading journeys pass in the Playwright E2E suite against the local stack.

story_outline.title: Order book view with live status
Cancel order from the order book
